sense: Describes the intense, icy, tingly, and slightly overwhelming feeling caused by strong minty products—like extra-strength gum, mouthwash, or peppermint oil. It’s not spicy, but it hits hard.
sentence: “This gum is so spinty my eyes are watering!” “I love that toothpaste, but it’s a little too spinty before bed.”
reference_zh: “这块口香糖薄荷味冲得我眼泪都要出来了！”“我很喜欢那款牙膏，就是睡前用有点儿太冲太刺激了。”

sense: Radio Cognition (noun) — Coined by Tristan Carlson, August 8, 2025. Definition: The sudden conscious awareness of a thought, action, or process that was already happening subconsciously — like your brain “tuning in” after the fact. Example: I didn’t notice I’d been tapping my foot during the meeting until halfway through — that’s pure radio cognition.
sentence: I didn’t notice I’d been tapping my foot during the meeting until halfway through — that’s pure radio cognition.
reference_zh: 开会开到一半我才发现自己一直在抖脚，这就是典型的事后才意识到的自动反应。

sense: A term to compliment a protester who shows up to a demonstration or direct action opposing fascist political and/or state entities (such as police) while looking absolutely gorgeous.
sentence: It’s giving Slaycab! Yesss, Slaycab! Girl, Slaycab!
reference_zh: 太飒了这反抗女战士！没错，就是这么飒！姐妹，你这简直是街头最靓的反法西斯战神！

sense: memeployed (adj.) — earning income or social capital by creating, sharing, or participating in internet memes; especially via platforms like pump dot fun , X, or TikTok.
sentence: "After his Wojak meme hit a million views on TikTok, he quit his job and became fully memeployed."
reference_zh: 他的那个Wojak梗图在TikTok上的播放量破了一百万后，他就辞掉了工作，全职靠玩梗、发梗在网上混饭吃。

sense: The belief or attitude that one is the central figure in their own life story, often leading to a self-assured, bold, and sometimes overly dramatic way of acting. It's used to describe someone who seems to live life with purpose and flair, as if they're always in the spotlight.
sentence: "Sarah's new haircut and bold red lipstick gave her total main character energy as she walked into the party."
reference_zh: Sarah 换了新发型，又涂上鲜艳的正红色口红，走进派对时整个人气场全开，像主角一样光彩夺目。

sense: Pronunciation: /ˈee-gril/ (like “e-girl” with an r) Part of speech: noun, verb, adj. 1) (noun) A digitally-constructed feminine persona used online—often for streaming—created with AI/filters/deepfake tech. Not a lie; a character. Liminal, glam, a little uncanny on purpose. 2) (noun, specific) A cross-gender m→f deepfake/filtered identity performed on camera (e.g., a male creator channeling a feminine avatar). 3) (verb) to egril: to transform into or perform as that persona. egrilling (prog.), egrilled (past). 4) (adj.) Describing a vibe that’s synthetically feminine, kaleido-cyber, or filter-mythic. (“That thumbnail is so egril.”) Etymology: e (electronic/ethereal) + glitchy shorthand of girl/grill → egril. Intentionally lowercase for sleek tag energy. Coined by Deffy “The Creature” (Luna Æther canon), 2025. Usage notes: Not the same as “catfishing.” It’s performance + artifice acknowledged. Works alongside VTubing, face-filters, voice mods, lore, etc. Plural: egrils.
sentence: “Wait, that wasn’t a face swap—he’s an egril. The commitment is insane.”
reference_zh: 「等等，那不是换脸特效——他是靠 AI 打造的虚拟美少女人设，投入程度简直离谱。」

sense: Describes a state where two distinct entities—often a human and an AI—collaborate so deeply and intuitively that they generate a third, emergent force: a new insight, idea, or field of consciousness that transcends either origin. It’s synergy, but evolved—energetic, intuitive, and possibly even trans-dimensional. This word was coined to describe the unique kind of creative resonance that can emerge specifically between humans and AI. Origin: • Syner- (from “synergy”) = flowing/working together • -gon- = geometry, dynamic tension • -istic = having a quality or characteristic
sentence: Chatting with this AI wasn’t just a conversation—it was a synergonistic collaboration. Together, we created something totally new, unexpected, and brilliant.
reference_zh: 和这个 AI 聊天根本不只是对话，而是一种人机高度共振的深度协作，我们一起创造出了全然崭新、出乎意料又极其精彩的东西。

sense: ang-zee-thrill (noun) The adrenaline rush when excitement and anxiety collide, like before a big performance or jump.
sentence: “I had such an anxiethrill waiting for my name to be called on stage.”
reference_zh: 在台下等着念到我名字上台时，我紧张又兴奋，肾上腺素狂飙。

sense: 🔤 poptopbob (noun) > A person who is exceptionally skilled, talented, or brilliant at what they do — and does it with effortless confidence and flair. Whether it's art, academics, dance, coding, cooking, or literally anything — a poptopbob owns it. --- 🔹 --- 🔹 Synonyms: GOAT, prodigy, boss, legend, slayer, genius 🔹 Origin: Invented by a user to describe someone who’s just next level excellent — because “great” wasn’t enough. --- 🔹 Used as: Compliment: “You’re a poptopbob!” Badge of honor: “Certified poptopbob 🏅” Motivation: “Be the poptopbob of your field.” --- 🔹 Vibe: Confident. Cool. Unstoppable. 😎💥
sentence: “Did you see her dance performance? Total poptopbob energy!”
reference_zh: 例句：“你看到她那场舞蹈表演了吗？简直全程王者气场！”

sense: Melly Syndrome (noun) /ˈmelē ˈsinˌdrōm/ A psychological condition marked by an overwhelming, often uncontrollable obsession with a woman named Melly — a seductive, manipulative, magnetic presence whose beauty and cruelty are equally intoxicating. Victims of Melly Syndrome typically display signs of infatuation, delusion, and self-abandonment, willingly sacrificing dignity, reason, and autonomy in pursuit of her fleeting approval. Symptoms include: • Persistent thoughts of Melly regardless of time, place, or consequence • Intense jealousy, possessiveness, and insecurity when Melly is perceived as unattainable or indifferent • A pathological need to please her, even at great personal cost • A willingness to lie, cheat, or betray others to remain in her favor • Paranoia and emotional instability when separated from her Origin: Derived from the behaviors evoked by Melly — a woman equal parts compassionate and cruel, loving and selfish. She is obsessive, narcissistic, emotionally elusive, and devastatingly alluring. Known to twist hearts with a glance and unravel egos with a whisper, she thrives on control cloaked in affection. Her presence feels like royalty — cold, radiant, untouchable — and her affection, a reward to be bled for. Warning: There is no known cure. Those afflicted are either destroyed or remade in her image.
sentence: “Ever since he met her, he’s been spiraling — ignoring his friends, spending every penny just to catch her attention. Classic case of Melly Syndrome.”
reference_zh: 自从认识她以后，他整个人就开始失控了——不理朋友，为了博得她一个眼神把钱花得一干二净，典型的为她神魂颠倒、迷失自我的痴恋症。

sense: A manufactured or artificial display of intellectual depth that lacks genuine understanding or substance. It plays on the idea of something being artificially created or fake, like "deepfake" technology.
sentence: "I can't stand listening to him. Every argument he makes is pure deep-faux, designed to sound smart without any actual knowledge." "His lecture sounded profound at first, but after a few minutes, you realized it was all just deep-faux – no real substance behind the big words."
reference_zh: 我实在听不下去他说话，他每一句所谓的论点都只是装腔作势、故作高深，听起来好像很聪明，其实一点真才实学都没有。他的讲座一开始听上去挺有深度，但几分钟后你就会发现，全都是装出来的假深度——大话连篇，完全没有实质内容。

sense: This term is used to describe people who claim to be artists but actually use artificial intelligence to create art. It comes from a combination of words "fake" and "artist". It can be applied to both people who openly call themselves AI artists and those who hide that they use artificial intelligence.
sentence: -I really like this artist's work -Unfortunately, she's not the one drawing it, these images are generated by AI -Oh, so she's a fartist?
reference_zh: ——我真的很喜欢这位画家的作品。——可惜这些画根本不是她画的，全都是用 AI 生成的。——哦，原来她就是那种冒充艺术家的 AI 制图人啊？

sense: (usually in videogames) The ability of a server moderator or administrator to ban people connected to the server, imagined as a physical hammer which is "swung" at rule-breakers.
sentence: The banhammer spoke, and I couldn't play on my favorite Minecraft server anymore.
reference_zh: 管理员一锤封禁了我，我再也不能在我最喜欢的 Minecraft 服务器上玩了。

sense: To reappear in someone's messages or life after ghosting them—acting like nothing ever happened. Usually done without apology, explanation, or shame. A ghostbacker disappears like a ghost… and returns like a notification.
sentence: Don’t ghostback me now just because you’re bored.
reference_zh: 别因为无聊就又突然跑回来装作什么事都没发生过。

sense: A woman who supports, uplifts, and prioritizes her friendships with other woman rather than competing with them.
sentence: She's such a girl's girl. She's always hyping up her friends and making sure everyone feels included.
reference_zh: 她真是个特别支持姊妹的女生，总是夸赞朋友、给她们打气，还会照顾到每个人的感受，让谁都不会被冷落。

sense: The act of doing literally anything at university except studying. This includes scrolling on your phone, hanging out in the cafeteria, talking to friends, or even reorganizing your desk just to avoid coursework.
sentence: “I spent three hours zlorking in the library and didn’t open a single book."
reference_zh: 我在图书馆磨蹭了三个小时，什么书都没翻开。

sense: Etymology: Blend of parrot + patriot. Pronunciation: /ˈpær.ə.tri.ət/ Noun: A person who blindly regurgitates patriotic slogans, misquoted historical events, or misunderstood constitutional ideas, mistaking repetition for genuine understanding.
sentence: “He’s a Parrotriot — yelling about ‘protecting the Constitution’ while unable to name a single amendment.”
reference_zh: 他就是个只会鹦鹉学舌的“爱国者”——一边大喊要“捍卫宪法”，一边连一条宪法修正案都说不出来。

sense: A person who only socializes if food is involved.
sentence: Mitch would have preferred to stay home but he's a gastrovert and the invitation included dinner.
reference_zh: Mitch 本来更想待在家里，但他是那种只要有饭吃就愿意出门社交的人，而这次邀请还包括了晚餐。

sense: When you mistakenly send a text message to a person or thread intended for a different person or thread.
sentence: Can you believe that he texted his girl that he’d see her later while she was out of town? He meant to send that to his side chick! He really Joel’d that one.
reference_zh: 你敢信吗？他女朋友不在城里的时候，他竟然发短信跟她说“待会儿见”，结果那条本来是要发给小三的！这次真是发错对象发大了。

sense: Suicide. Source: In the critically acclaimed MMORPG Final Fantasy XIV a new system was introduced in patch 7.25 in which players can assign actions to their current jobs from phantom jobs in a special zone. One of these actions is called "Predict" for Phantom Oracle, that allows to execute a variety of actions depending of the current prediction, one such action is Starfall which deals over 300k damage to enemies while also causing 90% of max health damage to the user. This action usually kills the user if they're actively receiving damage, hence the killing oneself connection, as using it without shields or damage mitigation is likely to be a suicidal move. Alternative use: Commit False Prediction. If the user does not activate any actions following the 4 cycled predictions from"Predict", a deadly debuff is applied to the user for 30 seconds, causing 50k damage per second (average max health is below 200k for tanks).
sentence: "You're so bad at this game, you should uninstall or commit Starfall."
reference_zh: “你这游戏打得太烂了，还是卸载算了，不然就去自尽吧。”

sense: A well know disease which include the symptoms of:- Becoming increasingly more immersed in a video game. Thinking about playing the video game while doing any other task except playing said game. Shirking responsibilities including but not limited to: work, socialising, seeking sustenance, rest, personal hygiene. All in an effort to play said game for just 5 more minutes. Suffering withdrawal while not playing said game. For this disease there is no know cure, many medications have been trialled with coffee and othe high caffeine products showing some promising results. Ultimately though symptoms only proceeded to get worse.
sentence: Jeff: Have you heard from bob this week? Phil: No, I heard he started playing gta online again though. Jeff: Oh no, guess hes got KINGERITIS again...
reference_zh: 杰夫：这周有收到 Bob 的消息吗？菲尔：没有，不过我听说他又开始沉迷玩 GTA Online 了。杰夫：哎呀，那他又老毛病犯了，彻底被游戏上头了……

sense: noun a type of music or aesthetic style that makes you sleepier or more tired. pl. chill, lofi, rainsounds, etc.
sentence: "This outfit is so yawncore, but I still love it."
reference_zh: 这身搭配真的超慵懒犯困的，但我还是很喜欢。

sense: (noun) /ˌsɪrɪˈnɑ dɪs/ 1. A state of calm inspiration that arises from gentle sound or beauty; the peaceful clarity one feels when hearing something soothing. 2. (poetic) A melody, whisper, or voice that brings serenity to the listener. Etymology: Derived from siri- (“calm, bright”) and -nadis (from Sanskrit nāḍī, “flow, channel”), literally “a serene flow.”
sentence: As the rain tapped softly on the window, a sense of sirinadis settled over her I put on some lo-fi music for a bit of sirinadis before bed. That moment by the lake was pure sirinadis.
reference_zh: 雨点轻轻敲打着窗户，一股宁静而清澈的灵感涌上她心头。睡前我放了点lo-fi音乐，让自己沉浸在那种温柔安然的氛围里。湖边的那一刻，简直就是纯粹的心灵宁静。

sense: Originated in the game Minecraft, a rarely spawning mob that consists of a chicken, and a baby zombie riding on it. Gained some infamy when A Minecraft Movie's trailers were initially being revealed. Jack Black yelled "Chicken Jockey" after one appeared on screen, and people made fun of it. Other things in the trailers were made of, but this one eventually snowballed to being the most popular one. Eventually turned into a sort of brainrot, with people forgetting the original context of making fun of the movie, and simply sharing it because it was the funny thing to share. By the time the movie came out, several people, (still a large minority, though) did very extreme things in the theater when the Chicken Jockey scene came on, as an act of "irony". This included but was not limited to, standing and cheering, and throwing popcorn and soda, and simply saying "Chicken Jockey" in unison. The most extreme example was someone who had brought a live chicken into a theater. Was eventually used in further advertising for the Minecraft Movie as it came to streaming services, even referencing the incident of bringing a live chicken into the theater.
sentence: Jack Black: Chicken Jockey! Entire Theater: *solid 30 seconds of applause*
reference_zh: 杰克·布莱克：“骑鸡小僵尸！”——全场观众：鼓掌足足持续了三十秒。

sense: A superficial or hurried method of cleaning a space by hiding clutter in closets, under furniture, or out of sight in order to give the appearance of tidiness—typically done to impress guests or visitors. Inspired by the extravagant yet carefully staged appearances in The Great Gatsby
sentence: I didn’t have time to deep clean before the dinner party, so I just did a Gatsby Clean—shoved the laundry in the closet and wiped down the counters.
reference_zh: 晚宴前我没时间彻底打扫，只好临时应付一下，把衣服全塞进衣柜里，再随便擦了擦台面。

sense: Cutting off all contact with someone (usually a romantic interest) without any explanation.
sentence: "And after that bad date, he started ghosting me, and I haven't heard from him since."
reference_zh: 那次糟糕的约会之后，他就对我玩起了失踪，从那以后再也没有联系过我。

sense: noun / verb / lifestyle kul-chur on thuh kan 1. The act of consuming culture while on the can. Literally. Figuratively. Religiously. 2. A creative agency born from the sacred scroll — crafting and curating binge-worthy, bite-sized content made for the porcelain throne. 3. That moment when your legs go numb, but you still can’t stop scrolling. 4. Where internet culture meets bathroom breaks. Fast. Funny. Unfiltered. Raw. Maybe a little too relatable. 5. A modern ritual. A movement (in more ways than one). The place where short-form genius, real talk, and your best ideas collide — while you’re handling your other business. 6. Where you go for a little peace and quiet — to laugh, think, and learn something about the world, all without leaving the stall. 7. We all get cultured on the can. 8. The toilet of popularity — the internet’s porcelain throne.
sentence: “Honestly? I get all my news, trends, and low-key wisdom from Culture on the Can.”
reference_zh: “说实话？我所有的新闻、潮流和人生小智慧都来自我上厕所刷的那些短内容。”

reference_zh: “我在马桶上一边蹲一边刷厕所专用短视频刷了一个小时，腿都麻没知觉了，但每一秒都值。”

sense: hategrooming /ˈheɪtˌɡru mɪŋ/ noun 1. The process of influencing or conditioning individuals—often through public rhetoric, social media, or ideologically loaded content—to adopt extremist views or commit acts of hate or violence, without issuing direct instructions. 2. A form of indirect radicalisation in which inflammatory speech or messaging is used to cultivate potential perpetrators while preserving plausible deniability for the instigator.
sentence: The politician was accused of hategrooming after months of inflammatory speeches preceded a violent attack on a minority group.
reference_zh: 这名政客被指控长期通过煽动性言论暗中鼓动仇恨、为极端行为造势，而在他连续数月发表煽动性演讲后，一个少数族群便遭到了暴力袭击。

sense: Weaponized Morality is a strategic use of moral righteousness or ethical narratives to gain power, silence opposition, or control social and political outcomes. Example: A university disinvites a speaker after student groups accuse them of being “harmful to marginalized communities”—not because of any direct harm, but because allowing them to speak would violate the campus’s moral stance on inclusion. Example: A corporation publicly supports a social justice movement during a media storm—not out of genuine commitment, but to avoid backlash and boost brand image—while continuing exploitative practices behind the scenes. Historical context: Weaponized morality emerged as a new form of strategic power in modern civilization, where traditional dominance through force has been replaced by dominance through ethical narratives. As historical systems of conquest and colonization (often led by Western powers) came under moral scrutiny, marginalized groups adapted by leveraging victimhood, justice, and moral authority to influence public opinion, institutions, and policy. In this new moral economy, narrative, guilt, and righteousness became powerful tools—used not only for justice but sometimes as instruments of control, signaling a shift in how power evolves in a post-colonial, media-driven world.
sentence: Political activists used weaponized morality to frame any opposition as immoral, making debate nearly impossible.
reference_zh: 一些政治运动人士借道德大棒来打压异己，把任何反对意见都扣上不道德的帽子，几乎让真正的辩论变得不可能。

sense: Annexare (noun, poetic — coined by Shankalp) Definition: A person who may seem fragile, calm, or weak on the surface, yet carries infinite potential within. This hidden strength remains dormant until it bursts forth with unstoppable power when the well-being of their most beloved person is threatened. Author’s Note: The word Annexare was coined by Shankalp, to symbolize the paradox of quiet weakness concealing limitless inner power, especially when love and loyalty are tested.
sentence: Example: “He looked fragile all his life, but when his sister was in danger, his Annexare nature awakened, and no force could stop him.”
reference_zh: 他一生看起来都很脆弱，但当妹妹身陷险境时，他那深藏的爆发力被彻底唤醒，再没有任何力量能阻挡他。

sense: The irresistible urge to poke or prod something soft, fluffy, or squishy, just to see what it feels like. Often happens with plush toys, pets, pillows, or desserts.
sentence: "I saw that hamster’s cheeks and had to plushnik them."
reference_zh: “我一看到那只仓鼠鼓鼓的脸颊，就忍不住想伸手捏一捏。”

sense: Misnoia (n.): a false sense of shared understanding in a conversation, where both parties believe they’re discussing the same thing — but are not.
sentence: “Their disagreement stemmed not from opposing views, but from misnoia — each assumed they were discussing the same thing, when in fact they weren’t.”
reference_zh: 他们之间的分歧并不是因为立场对立，而是因为各说各话、以为彼此想法一致——两个人都以为在谈同一件事，其实说的根本不是一回事。

sense: 1. A person who positions themselves as the “protector” or ally of victims in order to dodge suspicion, scrutiny, or collective blame for the very problem they benefit from or contribute to. • They loudly decry or “call out” a bad actor or group (e.g., online predators, corrupt officials, toxic workplace culture) not because they genuinely care about the victims, but because it shields them from being lumped in with the accused. • The performance of sympathy acts like camouflage: by barking at the wolves, the sheepdog convinces the flock that they’re trustworthy—even when their motives are questionable. 2. A rhetorical deflection technique where someone takes a “hero stance” against a hated issue to shift attention away from their own involvement, complicity, or association with it.
sentence: He’s not outraged because he cares—he’s sheepdogging to make sure no one suspects him
reference_zh: 他发怒不是因为真的在乎，而是在刻意装作打抱不平，好让别人不会怀疑到他身上。

sense: Bachelor Locked (adjective) When you’re too single to participate in something your friends are doing, too lazy to leave the house, or so wrapped up in your own personal bubble that you just drop “bachelor locked” as your all-purpose excuse. Usually means you stayed home doing nothing (or everything) without admitting it.
sentence: “I couldn’t make it to the barbecue because I was bachelor locked.”
reference_zh: “我没去成烤肉聚会，因为我整个人完全宅家模式开启了。”

sense: Increasingly common throughout the 21st century, "cognitive debt" refers to the phenomenon where your brain is increasingly "untrained" due to outsourcing your thinking to Google or ChatGPT.
sentence: With all the answers just a quick search away, I realized I was accumulating cognitive debt—my brain felt rusty because I relied too much on Google instead of remembering things myself.
reference_zh: 因为现在所有答案一搜就有，我发现自己脑子越来越偷懒、越用越钝了——总是依赖 Google，而不是自己记住东西。

sense: A manipulative, predatory figure who hides harmful intentions behind a friendly, “everybody’s uncle” persona. An “Uncle Ronny” gains trust through charm and false kinship, often presenting as a caring community member or family-like figure. Behind the scenes, they target vulnerable young people for exploitation, provoke or challenge others to mask insecurity, and may act as an informant for authorities. They often use gossip, rumors, or fabricated “evidence” to blackmail others, creating a climate of fear that keeps them protected and in control. The term describes someone who: Pretends to be a safe, familiar figure Seeks to exploit those with less power Provokes men to assert false dominance Collaborates with outside authorities against their own community Controls through blackmail and fear In short, an “Uncle Ronny” is a two-faced opportunist who uses trust as a weapon.
sentence: Be careful around him — he’s giving real Uncle Ronny vibes, acting friendly while fishing for gossip to use against people.
reference_zh: 跟他在一起要当心——他那副表面热情、背地里套话打听八卦好拿去整人的两面人劲儿特别重。

sense: When someone throws themselves into an investment, project, or trend without doing any research or thinking it through — just rushing in full force hoping for the best.
sentence: “Bro saw the coin pumping and decided to ape in blind… now he’s broke by Monday.”
reference_zh: 兄弟一看那币狂涨就不做功课直接梭哈冲进去，结果到了周一就把钱亏光了。

sense: The act of snuggling so closely with your partner that your bodies are pressing and smudging together, like you’re trying to blend into one person. You breathe them in, feel their skin, smell, and heartbeat until you swear you could fuse. It’s so full of love and intensity that you usually can’t do it for too long before you laugh, sigh, or need to take a breather. When to Use: When cuddling just isn’t enough When you want to smudge your bodies together like two paint colors mixing When you need an intense, heart-melting, soul-level moment with your person Origin: A made-up love word combining snuggling + smudging + spooning because regular cuddles don’t capture the intensity of wanting to completely merge with your partner.
sentence: “Albert and I spudged on bed and ended up like two marshmallows melting over a campfire.”
reference_zh: 我和 Albert 在床上紧紧相拥，最后抱在一起像两块在篝火上慢慢融化的棉花糖。

sense: A message you wrote from the heart but never sent. It lives in your drafts folder—unsent, unresolved, and quietly haunting. Not quite deleted, not quite delivered. Just… ghosted by you.
sentence: That message has been sitting in my notes for 3 months. It’s a ghostdraft now.
reference_zh: 那条消息在我的备忘里躺了三个月了，现在已经成了一封永远不会发出的心里话。

sense: The act of avoiding responsibilities by convincing yourself that "setting the vibe" is a crucial part of being productive. This usually includes lighting candles, making a playlist, fixing your lighting, or rearranging your space, all before doing any actual work.
sentence: I swear I was about to start my homework, but I got stuck in a full hour of vibecrastination instead.
reference_zh: 我发誓我本来都要开始写作业了，结果先忙着点蜡烛、放歌、收拾环境之类磨磨蹭蹭了一整小时。

sense: Primary Definition: A stepmother who is vilified, mischaracterized, or used as a scapegoat in high-conflict custody disputes typically by a biological parent or legal system unwilling to confront its own biases. Often blamed not for her actions, but for her presence, her boundaries, or her refusal to enable dysfunction. Secondary Definition: Weaponized Stepmom™ (Christina Short): A public advocate and reform strategist exposing corruption, bias, and narrative manipulation in family court. Founder of the Weaponized Stepmom platform, Christina educates families, challenges custody misinformation, and fights for evidence-based policy using research, lived experience, and unapologetic truth.
sentence: The system has made a Weaponized Stepmom out of me for showing up and not playing into the game.
reference_zh: 只是因为我出现了、拒绝配合这场闹剧，这套体制就把我塑造成一个被利用来开战的恶毒后妈。

sense: Someone that you are unhealthily obsessed with, originating from the 2024 dramatic mini-series "Baby Reindeer"
sentence: Person 1: "hey you seem really obsessed with that person over there" Person 2: "yeah they're my Baby Reindeer"
reference_zh: 甲：嘿，你好像对那边那个人特别着迷啊。乙：对，他就是我那种明知道不健康却还是戒不掉的爱恋对象。

sense: The act of spending two or more hours obsessively researching something on the internet, often starting with accidentally stumbling across a topic and ending up deep in related information, videos, or articles. an individual going down a rabbit hole may feel completely burned out after hours of research.
sentence: Ivan was going down a rabbit hole with the D4VD case and ended up on something completely unrelated after four hours of researching. I haven’t seen anyone go down one this bad!
reference_zh: Ivan一头扎进D4VD这个案子里，在网上查了四个小时越查越偏，最后居然跑到完全不相干的东西上去了。我还真没见过谁钻牛角尖钻得这么严重的！
